DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF MOHAMMED ALCOZAI'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY AFTER ARBITRATION AWARD PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MAY NOTICE THE DEPOSITIONS OF DEFENDANTS PEREZ AND CENTURY WINDOW CLEANING ONLY. PLAINTIFF MAY PROPOUND ONE SET OF FOLLOW UP INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS. PLAINTIFF MUST COMPLETE DISCOVERY BY THE DISCOVERY CUTOFF DATE SO AS NOT TO REQUIRE A TRIAL CONTINUANCE. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL HAS PROVIDED A DECLARATION OF ATTORNEY FAULT. THE ADDITIONAL LIMITED DISCOVERY WILL NOT CAUSE A DELAY IN THE TRIAL. WHILE THERE IS NO EXCUSE FOR PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL'S NOT KNOWING THE LAW, THERE IS NO REASON THAT PLAINTIFF SHOULD BE PREJUDICED BY HIS COUNSEL'S ERROR. FURTHERMORE, DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT PROVIDED ANY EVIDENCE IN THE OPPOSITION BY WAY OF DECLARATION OR OTHER EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THEIR POSITION ("PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL HAS NOT BEEN DILIGENT IN CONDUCTING DISCOVERY IN THIS CASE...") PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: PETER CATALANOTTI; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Re-Open Discovery After Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Jul-20-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
PLAINTIFF MOHAMMED ALCOZAI's Motion To Re-Open Discovery After Arbitration Award Plaintiff's Motion to Reopen Discovery is GRANTED. Plaintiff may notice the depositions of Defendants Perez and Century Window Cleaning only. Plaintiff may propound one set of follow up interrogatories and requests for admissions. Plaintiff must complete discovery by the discovery cutoff date so as not to require a trial continuance. Plaintiff's counsel has provided a declaration of attorney fault. The additional limited discovery will not cause a delay in the trial. While there is no excuse for plaintiff's counsel's not knowing the law, there is no reason that plaintiff should be prejudiced by his counsel's error. Furthermore, defendants have not provided any evidence in the Opposition by way of declaration or other evidence to support their position ("Plaintiff's counsel has not been diligent in conducting discovery in this case...") Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not reported. =302/jpt
